note: Template only. Create fresh jobs in the target environment rather than copying IDs or runtime state.

payload.message: Executive assistant sweep. Use the existing executive-assistant skill. This cron is the dependable trigger for recurring EA work. Rely on the existing skill for the detailed sweep procedure. If the sweep finds partner / referral / prospect work, also apply the existing business-development skill. Keep the run bounded and deterministic.

Run the default CT prospecting batch. Use the existing business-development skill. This cron is the dependable trigger for the recurring BD batch. Rely on the existing skill for the detailed procedure. Keep the run bounded and deterministic.

Prepare today's tasks in Todoist only. Use the existing daily-task-prep skill. This cron is the dependable trigger for morning task curation. Rely on the existing skill for the detailed procedure. Keep the run bounded and deterministic.
Executive assistant sweep. Use the existing executive-assistant skill. This optional boundary job is useful when your desired sweep window cannot be expressed cleanly in one cron expression.

In your OpenClaw repo, perform the nightly backup to the configured Git remote. Use a deterministic allowlisted git flow instead of exploratory checks: stage only the restore-critical tracked paths you intend to back up, check whether the staged diff is empty, and only commit / push when it is not. Do not add secrets, runtime state, logs, media, browser state, or ignored files. If push fails or something needs human attention, send a short proactive update. Otherwise stay silent.

Check whether an OpenClaw update is available. If there is an update, install it using the gateway update tool. If there is no update, do nothing. Keep this run silent unless there is a meaningful problem that needs human attention.
